meta.note: Tunisia + Syria + Iraq sweep, published books. Thin title-level records (mostly author/category-level in sources). Held: 'Have You Heard the Talk of the Rafida?' (no author; Zarqawi-audio compilation; an Iraq-requested removal at the 2026 Damascus fair — surfaced in both SY and IQ research). Syria bans recorded historical (lifted after the Dec 2024 fall of Assad). Sources: Al Jazeera, Nawaat, MEMO, The New Arab, Enab Baladi, Wikipedia.

entries.title: The Tunisian Frankenstein
entries.description_ban: Satirical essays casting President Kaïs Saïed as a Frankenstein's monster; security forces seized copies from Dar el-Kitab's stand at the 37th Tunis International Book Fair (28 April 2023) and closed the stand.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; reported by Al Jazeera and Middle East Eye.

entries.title: Kais the First, President of a Drunken Ship
entries.description_ban: Critical portrait of President Kaïs Saïed; at the 2023 Tunis book fair, security officers took samples and asked the publisher to withdraw remaining copies, despite the book having been displayed the two prior years.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair restriction; reported by Al Jazeera.

entries.title: Rached Ghannouchi and the Islamic Ennahdha in Tunisia
entries.description_ban: Police reportedly confiscated this title about Ennahdha leader Rached Ghannouchi at the December 2021 Tunis book fair; the fair director denied confiscations (conflicting accounts).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair confiscation; reported by Nawaat (medium confidence).

entries.title: L'assassinat de Salah Ben Youssef
entries.description_ban: Investigation implicating Bourguiba's circle in the 1961 assassination of nationalist leader Salah Ben Youssef; banned under Ben Ali and only reappearing in Tunisian bookstores after the 2011 revolution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: Le Livre noir (Le Système de propagande sous Ben Ali)
entries.description_ban: State dossier naming media figures who collaborated with Ben Ali's propaganda machine; a Tunis court banned its printing and distribution after a defamation suit, upheld on appeal in July 2014.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban of a state-published exposé; reported by Middle East Monitor (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Shell
entries.description_ban: Autobiographical prison novel (al-Qawqaa) depicting torture in Hafez al-Assad's Tadmur prison; could not be published in Syria and was openly described as banned. Lifted after the December 2024 fall of Assad.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by The New Arab and Enab Baladi.

entries.title: My Aunt's House
entries.description_ban: Prison-literature novel about women in Sednaya prison ('my aunt's house' = Syrian slang for prison); described as among the most fiercely banned books under Assad, now openly sold. Exact ban year unknown (proxy). Lifted after December 2024.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by Enab Baladi and Syria Times.

entries.title: Rue Ben Barka
entries.description_ban: Novel banned by Iraqi government censorship in 1970; it could not be published in Iraq and appeared 15+ years later in Egypt, Jordan and Beirut. Saeed was an Iraqi political prisoner under the Baath regime.

entries.title: Rhythm and Obsession
entries.description_ban: Iraqi government censorship blocked publication of this novel in 1968; it first appeared in Syria decades later. Part of the blanket Iraqi ban on Saeed's publications (1963–2008).
